next_steps: Excisional biopsy of an enlarged lymph node for histopathology, immunohistochemistry, and T-cell receptor gene rearrangement studies
Peripheral blood flow cytometry for Sézary cells
Skin biopsy (repeat) with focus on immunohistochemistry (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD25, CD30, FoxP3)
PET-CT scan to guide biopsy of the most metabolically active pulmonary or nodal lesion
T-cell receptor gene rearrangement analysis from peripheral blood
Continue broad-spectrum antimicrobial coverage pending definitive culture results and biopsy pathology

differential_diagnoses: Drug Reaction with Eosinophilia and Systemic Symptoms (DRESS) syndrome
Primary cutaneous aggressive epidermotropic CD8+ cytotoxic T-cell lymphoma
Disseminated herpes simplex infection (Kaposi's varicelliform eruption) with superinfection
next_steps: Excisional lymph node biopsy for histopathology, immunohistochemistry, and T-cell receptor gene rearrangement studies
Peripheral blood flow cytometry for aberrant T-cell populations (Sézary cells)
Repeat skin biopsies from ulcerative and non-ulcerative sites for histopathology and immunohistochemistry (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD25, CD30, FoxP3, PD-1, CXCL13)
Bronchoscopic biopsy or fine-needle aspiration of pulmonary nodules
Serum testing for HTLV-1, EBV viral load, and soluble IL-2 receptor levels
Skin and tissue cultures for mycobacteria, fungi, and atypical bacteria
Complete medication reconciliation to assess for potential DRESS-triggering agents
